key_insight: FormalAnalyticGeo turns analytic geometry problem generation into a closed-loop neural-symbolic pipeline by using a formal intermediate language (CDL) to guarantee diagram- and answer-consistent outputs.
engineering_relevance: It provides a scalable way to synthesize high-quality multimodal geometry datasets, which is valuable for training and evaluating vision-language systems on math reasoning.
method: Generator LLM creates diverse analytic geometry problems.
Formalizer converts each problem into CDL for precise SDF-based diagram rendering.
Measurer and Quality Verifier check rendered diagrams and extracted answers, with automatic retries in a feedback loop.
evidence_signal: The resulting AnalyticGeo7K dataset contains over 7K verified multimodal problems, with a median ground-truth relative error of 0.70% and 82.3% of answers within 5% of the exact symbolic solution.
watch_item: The pipeline depends on accurate formalization and vision-based measurement, so errors in CDL conversion or rendering/measurement could propagate despite the verification loop.
paper_title: FormalAnalyticGeo: A Neural-Symbolic Based Framework for Multimodal Analytic Geometry Problem Generation
